name: Starton Holdings, Inc.
business: We are a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on improving standard-of-care therapies for people with cancer through application of our proprietary platform of delivery technology systems. Our lead program, STAR-LLD, is a continuous delivery system of lenalidomide for hematologic malignancies. We are pursuing multiple indications in multiple myeloma and chronic lymphocytic leukemia, and potentially expanding into other cancers.

exposures.sentence: We are a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on improving standard-of-care therapies for people with cancer through application of our proprietary platform of delivery technology systems.
Our first subcutaneous infusion system pump is a battery-operated, commercially available medical device.

Starton is actively planning to fund several IIT studies at certain major academic centers in the United States in lymphoma and multiple myeloma in patients who have progressed following CAR-T therapy with lymphoma and in RRMM patients in combination with CAR-T for the first 30-days after the infusion to improve the biochemical response to treatment over CAR-T alone.

catalysts.event: Start of IIT studies for STAR-LLD-SC in post-CAR-T patients
catalysts.sentence: These studies, which are expected to start at the end of Q2/2026, will provide insight into the mechanisms and success rate of STAR LLD -SC in each population.
We expect to announce our second program, which is currently in the formulation development stage, within the next 12 to 18 months.

catalysts.event: Commercialization of STAR-LLD following Revlimid patent expiration
catalysts.sentence: since Revlimid ® is under IP protection until April of 2028 in the U.S., we plan to commercialize STAR -LLD-SC and STAR -LLD-OCR after the expiration of the Revlimid patents, subject to receiving all required regulatory approvals.

tamClaims.claim: Revlimid had global revenues of $12.8 billion in 2022
tamClaims.sentence: The primary first line agent, REVLIMID ® (lenalidomide), is the standard of care in MM in the U.S. and during 2022 it had global revenues of $12.8 billion U.S. dollars based on disclosures in Bristol Myers Squibb public filings with the SEC.

tamClaims.claim: CLL and MM are the most common blood cancers in the US with 20,700 and 35,780 new diagnoses annually
tamClaims.sentence: CLL and MM are the most common blood cancers in the US with 20,700 and 35,780 new diagnoses expected annually, respectively (American Cancer Society 2024).
CLL, with a prevalence of roughly 200,000 patients represents the most common form of leukemia in the U.S.
